behaviors.description: The evaluate-stage command stores grounding into a dedicated `grounding` artifact, not the `plan` artifact.
behaviors.justification: Real failure is at a module handoff: evaluate-stage writer -> artifact store key/slot mapping. A unit test of a pure mapper can miss wiring mistakes (wrong repository call, wrong artifact type constant). Rejected higher levels: no behavior text requires real OS/network/subprocess, and correctness is about destination artifact key, not LLM judgment quality.
behaviors.test_description: Run evaluate-stage command against a real in-process artifact repository (test DB or filesystem-backed store). Assert a new `grounding` artifact is created, `plan` is not written by evaluate, and metadata/type are persisted under the run context.

behaviors.description: The implement-stage flow reads grounding when available and still writes its execution output to `plan`.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is implement orchestration across modules: artifact read path + implement output write path. This is not a single-function assertion because bugs often appear in repository wiring/order of operations. Rejected Agentic: behavior text checks read/write routing ('reads grounding', 'writes ... to `plan`'), not whether model reasoning is good.
behaviors.test_description: Seed run context with a `grounding` artifact in an in-process store, invoke implement flow with deterministic model/tool stubs, then assert implement consumed grounding input and emitted execution output to `plan` without mutating `grounding`.

behaviors.description: Deep-dive no longer overwrites `plan` as a redundant side effect when diagnosis already exists in issue content.
behaviors.justification: This is side-effect control across deep-dive stage + issue-content reader + artifact writer. Real regression is unintended write during module interaction, not pure algorithm logic. Rejected System/Agentic: no required real OS/network, and condition is deterministic ('diagnosis already exists in issue content').
behaviors.test_description: Provide issue content containing diagnosis and a preexisting `plan` artifact in test storage; run deep-dive stage; assert no new write/update to `plan` occurs and prior `plan` version/hash remains unchanged.

behaviors.description: Artifact lineage is preserved so implement can read the `grounding` artifact produced earlier in the same run context.
behaviors.justification: Failure appears in cross-module state handoff (run-context lineage, artifact indexing/version linkage). Needs end-to-end module wiring in-process. Rejected Workflow: behavior text is lineage/addressability, not multi-agent decision quality; can be validated deterministically without real model judgments.
behaviors.test_description: Within one test run context, execute evaluate write of `grounding`, then implement artifact lookup using real lineage/index modules. Assert implement resolves the exact prior `grounding` artifact (id/version/context match) and not artifacts from other contexts.

behaviors.description: In a full multi-step agent run (`deep-dive -> write-plan/evaluate -> implement`), the final implementation plan reflects grounding produced earlier in the run.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly requires a full multi-step agent chain and that downstream plan content reflects earlier grounding. This depends on sequential agentic handoff and model-mediated use of prior artifact content; stubs can hide real failures in how the model uses grounding. Rejected lower levels: text explicitly says 'full multi-step agent run'.
behaviors.test_description: Execute the real pipeline `deep-dive -> write-plan/evaluate -> implement` in one run context with realistic prompts and model calls enabled. Assert final `plan` includes key grounding-derived facts/constraints produced earlier (using rubric/invariants), and lineage shows grounding source from same run.

behaviors.description: When grounding is absent, implement falls back safely and still emits a valid plan artifact without corrupting other stored artifacts.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is fallback orchestration with shared artifact store: missing-read path, default behavior, and no collateral writes. Needs module interaction, not real infra. Rejected Agentic: behavior checks safe fallback and artifact integrity, not quality of model output decisions.
behaviors.test_description: Start run context with no `grounding` artifact and with unrelated existing artifacts. Run implement flow in-process; assert a valid `plan` artifact is created, no exceptions leak, and unrelated artifacts remain byte-for-byte/metadata unchanged.
